danger_signals.visual_form: Colored ghost sprites (red/Blinky, pink/Pinky, cyan/Inky, orange/Sue) moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player life; contact causes loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Ghosts moving in coordinated patterns with semi-random movement preventing predictable evasion
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger as ghosts employ learned behavioral patterns to corner Ms. Pac-Man

danger_signals.visual_form: Faster sprite animation and movement velocity of both player and ghosts across progressive rounds
danger_signals.meaning: Difficulty escalation; world state becoming more hostile and demanding

danger_signals.visual_form: Power pellets provide shorter vulnerability windows for ghosts as rounds progress, eventually ceasing to work
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of escape mechanism; player becomes increasingly vulnerable

safety_signals.visual_form: Large pellet sprite; ghosts turn blue and reverse direction
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability window; threat reversal from hunted to hunter

safety_signals.visual_form: Passageways at maze edges allowing teleportation across screen
safety_signals.meaning: Escape route; allows evasion of pursuing ghosts

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid-color walls with distinct outline vs. path contrast (improvement over original)
safety_signals.meaning: Visual clarity reduces navigation confusion and increases player agency

safety_signals.visual_form: Fruit sprites bouncing through maze (cherries, banana, etc.) appearing twice per round
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity for point gain and progression; safe to pursue when ghost threat is managed

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Female character design; bow or dress visual distinction from original Pac-Man
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Centered starting position in maze; controls primary movement through pellet collection

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Four distinct colored ghosts with consistent visual identity across gameplay
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ghosts turn blue when powered-down (energizer active), indicating temporary role reversal
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emerge from center ghost box; patrol specific behavioral zones (Blinky/Pinky random then patterned; Inky/Sue use corner-targeting patterns)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pink-colored maze; slower ghost speed; longer power pellet vulnerability duration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Blinky and Pinky move semi-randomly for first several seconds before establishing patterns; player has extended escape window

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Maze color scheme shifts from light blue (levels 3-5) to brown (levels 6-9); increasing animation speed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ghosts establish patterns faster; power pellet duration shortens; difficulty escalates through speed and reduced invulnerability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark blue maze; maximum speed; minimal or no power pellet protection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ghosts become nearly unstoppable; player must rely on perfect pattern execution; energizers may cease functioning entirely

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ms. Pac-Man spins or swoons, falling in defeat animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Life counter decrements; ghost positions reset; player respawns at maze center

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Narrative cutscenes showing relationship progression between Pac-Man and Ms. Pac-Man (meeting → romance → baby arrival)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay pauses; world-state contextualized through character relationship storytelling

environmental_storytelling.technique: Maze color coding by progression stage
environmental_storytelling.example: Pink maze (early), light blue (mid), brown (late), dark blue (endgame) creates visual language of escalating difficulty without UI explanation
Bouncing fruits that traverse warp tunnels communicate dynamic world where collectibles are mobile targets, increasing challenge and visual interest
Four distinct ghost colors (Blinky red/aggressive, Pinky pink/predictive, Inky cyan/evasive, Sue orange/unpredictable) create implied behavioral identities without exposition
Three intermissions depicting Pac-Man/Ms. Pac-Man romance arc contextualize the game within larger franchise narrative; character relationship progression provides meta-world-state
Improvement from original: solid-color walls vs. outline style communicates design learning and player accessibility without explicit tutorial

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Semi-random movement for first several seconds before establishing pursuit pattern
Aggressive pursuit pattern; targets player directly
Predictive pathing; attempts to intercept player movement
Movement pattern targeting toward designated corner; less aggressive than Blinky/Pinky
Pattern-based corner-targeting; behavioral continuity from original Pac-Man (Clyde)
Color shift to blue; reverse direction immediately; flee from player; become vulnerable to consumption for bonus points
Speed reduction applied; provides temporary escape window for player
Continuous movement through maze; points accumulate; progression toward round completion
Spin/swoon animation; fall; life loss; respawn at center

notes: Ms. Pac-Man (1993 NES Namco version) uses minimalist visual communication typical of early 1980s arcade design. Danger and safety are signaled through ghost color, sprite animation speed, and maze color-coding by progression stage. The game lacks explicit UI elements for world-state communication; instead, visual changes to maze aesthetics and ghost behavior convey difficulty escalation. The four-ghost faction is not a traditional 'faction' in narrative terms, but rather a unified antagonistic force with differentiated behavioral personalities. Intermission sequences provide meta-narrative context within the Pac-Man universe. The improvement of solid maze walls over outline rendering demonstrates design iteration focused on player clarity. NES conversion maintains arcade design philosophy with minimal adaptation.
